_instructions.usage: Copy this file to clients/[slug].input.json, fill in all fields, remove this _instructions block, then run: npx ts-node scripts/deploy.ts --input clients/[slug].input.json
_instructions.prerequisite_railway: Before running: create a Railway project in the client's account, add 3 services named exactly 'Sera Worker', 'Sera Dashboard', 'Sera API', generate railway.app domains for Sera API and Sera Dashboard. Then generate an ACCOUNT-LEVEL token (railway.app/account/tokens while logged in as the client's Railway account). NOTE: Project tokens (Project Settings > Tokens) are deployment-only and will not work for CLI provisioning. Use an account token.
_instructions.slug: kebab-case client identifier, e.g. verdana-commons. Used for manifest filename and progress file.
_instructions.clientName: Human-readable client name, e.g. 'Verdana Commons'. Used in summaries, manifest, and step headers. Can include spaces.
_instructions.tenantId: Short uppercase identifier written to Processing Events, e.g. VERDANA. Typically slug uppercased with underscores.
_instructions.emailSetupType: How the capture inbox is set up. Choose one of: 'saberra-gmail' (Saberra-owned Gmail or Google Workspace account, most common), 'client-gmail' (client's own Gmail or Google Workspace), 'other-imap' (non-Google provider like Outlook/M365 or Fastmail - uses IMAP password), 'new-workspace' (new Google Workspace account needs to be created as an add-on service). Determines IMAP auth path and what guidance is shown. Leave empty to be prompted interactively.
_instructions.notionParentPageId: ID from the Notion page URL where the databases will be created. Can paste full URL - the wizard will extract the ID. Create a blank page named '[Client] Living Memory Hub' and share it with the Notion integration first. Most common failure: valid key but page not shared with integration.
_instructions.notionApiKey: From notion.so/my-integrations - create a new integration for this client workspace, copy the Internal Integration Secret (starts with secret_), share the parent page with it.
_instructions.rootsEmail: The email address that receives incoming mail for the client (what Google Meet notifications and forwarded emails are sent to). For Saberra-managed aliases (e.g. verdana@saberra.com), this is the alias address - but note imapUser must be the mailbox OWNER (e.g. systems@saberra.com). For non-Google/Proton Mail clients, set this to a Gmail/Workspace forwarding address.
_instructions.adminEmail: Client human inbox for review alerts and access request notifications. Must differ from rootsEmail to avoid email processing loops.
_instructions.imapHost: IMAP server hostname. Gmail/Google Workspace: imap.gmail.com | Outlook/M365: outlook.office365.com | Fastmail: imap.fastmail.com. NOTE: Proton Mail requires Bridge (a local daemon) and is NOT compatible with cloud-hosted workers - use forwarding to Gmail/Workspace instead.
_instructions.imapUser: The IMAP authentication username. CRITICAL: this must be the MAILBOX OWNER, not an alias. If rootsEmail is verdana@saberra.com (an alias that routes to systems@saberra.com), set imapUser to systems@saberra.com. For direct mailboxes with no aliasing, imapUser equals rootsEmail.
_instructions.imapPassword: For non-Google providers only (emailSetupType='other-imap'). App password, not the account password. Gmail/Workspace: leave blank - OAuth is used instead. Outlook/M365: account.microsoft.com > Security > App passwords. Fastmail: Settings > Passwords and Security > App passwords.
_instructions.googleClientId: From Google Cloud Console - OAuth 2.0 Client ID (Desktop app type). Must be created while logged in as the capture inbox account (or systems@ for Saberra-managed). APIs to enable: Drive v3, Docs v1, Gmail v1, Sheets, Slides.
_instructions.googleClientSecret: From Google Cloud Console - OAuth 2.0 Client Secret.
_instructions.googleRefreshToken: Run: npx ts-node scripts/google-auth.ts --client-id YOUR_ID --client-secret YOUR_SECRET then paste the printed GOOGLE_REFRESH_TOKEN value here. If no token is returned, a previous authorization exists - revoke it at myaccount.google.com/permissions then re-run. This token never expires unless revoked.
_instructions.governingPurpose: Optional. Client's Governing Purpose Statement - the full text. Sera scores every decision and task against this statement for purpose alignment. Leave empty string if none.
_instructions.communityLayer: true to create all 24 databases including Tensions/Commitments/Gratitudes/Events/Retrospectives/Resources. false for core 18 only.
_instructions.claudeModel: Leave as claude-sonnet-4-6 unless client is on enterprise tier.
_instructions.sensitiveReviewParentPageId: Optional but strongly recommended for production. ID of a page in a SEPARATE admin-only Notion workspace where the Sensitive Review database will be created. If omitted, Sensitive Review is created in the main hub page - which is less secure.
_instructions.railwayToken: Account-level Railway token for the client's Railway account. Generate from: railway.app/account/tokens (while logged in as the CLIENT's Railway account). This is different from a project token. Written here for provisioning only - never commit this file.
_instructions.railwayProjectId: The Railway project ID. Found in the project URL: railway.app/project/PROJECT_ID or in project Settings.
_instructions.seraApiUrl: The railway.app domain for the Sera API service, e.g. https://sera-api-production-xxxx.up.railway.app. Generate in Railway Dashboard: Sera API service > Settings > Networking > Generate Domain.
_instructions.seraDashboardUrl: The railway.app domain for the Sera Dashboard service, e.g. https://dashboard-production-xxxx.up.railway.app. Generate in Railway Dashboard: Sera Dashboard service > Settings > Networking > Generate Domain.
